title: Gemini for Google Workspace Professionals

modules.title: What Gemini in Workspace Actually Does: Capabilities, Limits, and Where It Lives
modules.sub: Mapping Gemini's real presence across your Workspace environment
modules.description: Learners take stock of where Gemini actually appears inside Gmail, Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Meet, and what it can and cannot do in each context. They distinguish marketing claims from functional reality so they can set accurate expectations before building any workflow. By the end, they have a clear mental map of Gemini's surface area inside Workspace.

modules.title: Practical Workflows: Docs, Gmail, Sheets, Slides, and Meet in the Real World
modules.sub: Hands-on Gemini use across five core Workspace tools
modules.description: Learners work through concrete, task-based scenarios in each Workspace application — drafting and summarizing in Gmail, structuring documents in Docs, generating and cleaning data in Sheets, building decks in Slides, and using meeting assistance in Meet. They apply Gemini features to realistic professional tasks rather than toy examples. The focus is on identifying which Gemini actions produce genuine time savings in their own day-to-day work.

modules.title: Prompt Craft for Workspace Contexts: Getting Useful Output from Ambiguous Inputs
modules.sub: Writing prompts that reliably produce usable, context-aware output
modules.description: Learners examine why generic prompts fail inside Workspace tools and practice rewriting them with appropriate context, constraints, and framing. They test prompting patterns against real work artifacts — existing documents, email threads, and data sets — to evaluate output quality. They leave with a personal prompt toolkit tuned to the kinds of tasks they actually perform.

modules.title: Data Privacy, Organizational Controls, and What Your Workspace Admin Sees
modules.sub: Understanding privacy boundaries before sharing sensitive work with Gemini
modules.description: Learners examine how Google handles data submitted to Gemini in Workspace, including what is retained, reviewed, or used for model training under different account configurations. They review the controls administrators can set and how those settings affect what individual users can and cannot do. The goal is confident, informed use rather than avoidance — learners finish knowing exactly which data is safe to include in a prompt and which is not.

modules.title: Building Your Personal Productivity System Around Gemini Integration
modules.sub: Designing a repeatable, Gemini-augmented workflow you will actually use
modules.description: Learners audit their current work patterns and identify the highest-leverage points where Gemini assistance fits without creating new friction. They design and document a personal workflow that integrates the prompting techniques, privacy boundaries, and tool-specific behaviors covered in earlier modules. The module closes with a commitment plan — specific triggers, templates, and habits that make the new workflow stick after the course ends.

synopsis: Gemini is embedded directly into the Google Workspace tools most professionals already spend their day in — but most users interact with it superficially, missing the workflows that actually save time. This course cuts through the feature announcements and delivers a hands-on system for using Gemini across Gmail, Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Meet to produce real work faster.
rationale: Google Workspace is one of the most widely deployed productivity platforms in the world, and Gemini's deep integration means that professionals who master it gain a compounding daily advantage — yet most training treats it as a novelty rather than a workflow tool.
learning_outcome: Professionals can build and apply a personal Gemini-augmented workflow across their core Workspace tools, including appropriate use of privacy controls and prompting techniques that produce reliable, useful output.
nearest_existing: Gemini's Origins: From LaMDA to the Multimodal Era
